acceptance_criteria: `agent_runtime.capture_workspace_checkpoint` is registered on the agent-runtime queue and nowhere else.
New capture requests use `ManagedWorkspaceLocator`, not arbitrary absolute workspace paths.
The activity resolves the workspace through the managed run store and verifies runtime/run/workflow/step correlation.
A managed workspace is never passed to `TemporalSandboxActivities` for capture.
`worktree_archive` capture includes tracked and untracked files, binary files, executable bits, and safe symlink metadata.
Runtime credentials, provider homes, control sockets, caches, and out-of-repository state are excluded.
The archive and manifest are deterministic enough for stable digest and retry verification.
Large payloads remain in artifacts; workflow history receives compact refs and summaries only.
Capture is idempotent across activity retries and concurrent duplicate invocations.
A successful primary execution remains durable when managed capture fails.
The canonical Step Execution checkpoint writer can consume the managed capture result and persist a boundary checkpoint ref.
Capture-only support does not make checkpoint Resume eligible.
Existing sandbox and external-state checkpoint paths continue to use their own owners and policies.
Existing Temporal histories remain replayable through an explicit patch/version cutover.
source_resolution: Full issue content recovered through authenticated GitHub issue tooling.
title: [Checkpointing P0] Implement codex_cli managed workspace checkpoint capture
